title: Nageia coriacea
intro: Nageia coriacea, also known as the Philippine pine, is a species of evergreen coniferous tree native to the Philippines. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its potential to treat fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, making it a promising natural remedy for various health conditions. Bioactive constituents of Nageia coriacea include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Nageia coriacea is commonly used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Nageia coriacea is classified as a species of coniferous tree in the family Podocarpaceae. It belongs to the genus Nageia, which is part of the order Podocarpales. This species is native to Southeast Asia and is a member of the family Podocarpaceae, which is a group of evergreen conifers. The family Podocarpaceae is a part of the broader class Pinopsida, which includes all conifers. Nageia coriacea is further classified into the kingdom Plantae, indicating its status as a plant species.
uses: Nageia coriacea has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian cultures for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of various health issues. The plant's bark and leaves are used in traditional medicine to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The extracts from Nageia coriacea have been found to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which may contribute to its therapeutic effects. However, more research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal uses and potential benefits. Traditional healers continue to rely on Nageia coriacea for its perceived health benefits, often using it in combination with other plant-based remedies.
benefits: Nageia coriacea is a species of coniferous tree, and while limited information is available about its health benefits, traditional uses of its extracts and essential oils may have potential applications in aromatherapy and as a natural remedy for stress relief. The potential health benefits of Nageia coriacea are largely anecdotal and require further scientific investigation.

parts: Nageia coriacea, commonly known as the Damar or Yellow Meranti, is a species of coniferous tree native to Southeast Asia. The bark of Nageia coriacea is traditionally used in the treatment of fever and other health issues. The sapwood of this tree is also used to produce a resin, which can be used as a treatment for skin and wound infections. In addition, the leaves and seeds of Nageia coriacea are used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The medicinal properties of Nageia coriacea are primarily attributed to its high content of antioxidants and other bioactive compounds.
